source: .claude/skills/ir:onboard-agent/scenario-meanings.md → basic-turn (repeated three times in one interactive session)
notes: Three consecutive user prompts in ONE live aider REPL session, validating that state resets to ready between turns — ready→working→ready three times on the SAME session — and that the session survives all three turns as a single live entity (one proc-<PID>), then tears down once. WHY interactive (not headless `aider --message`): headless aider is single-shot and exits after one reply, so it can't show turn-to-turn state reset; the aider interactive driver (drive-aider-interactive.sh) keeps the REPL alive across all three turns via send+wait_turn and kills the tmux session once at the end → a SINGLE process_exited, not three. AIDER TURN-END IS SYNTHESIZED, NOT MARKED: the markdown transcript has NO turn-end marker — a `> Tokens:` line closes one model CALL (assistant_message), not the turn (under --yes-always aider may re-prompt within one turn). End-of-turn is synthesized OUT-OF-BAND by IdleFlush after aiderIdleTurnDoneAfter (1500ms) quiet, but the daemon's SessionDetector polls working sessions every 5s, so the EFFECTIVE working→ready latency after a turn's last line is ~5s. The recipe therefore uses generous inter-turn / trailing sleeps (≥7s) so each turn's working→ready flip fires (IdleFlush) AND is observed (next poll) before the next prompt re-enters working; with shorter sleeps the next `####` prompt lands before IdleFlush and the parser opens the new turn WITHOUT an intervening turn_done — the session stays working across the boundary and the per-turn reset is lost (TestParser_NewUserPromptAfterMultiCall_StaysWorking; the captured regression run shows exactly this — one ready→working, no per-turn flips). LAZY TRANSCRIPT: .aider.chat.history.md (FilesUnderCWD) materializes only once the first user input lands, so the live REPL is observable from launch as a transient proc-<PID> pre-session born ready (the process scanner) before the per-cwd transcript appears — session_birth, which only asserts 'a session appears in ready within 2s of launch', legitimately matches that pre-session. The TURN ARCS, by contrast, must all land on the session that goes working: t1_start anchors to recording start with NO session pin so the matcher picks the FIRST working in the whole recording (the pre-session never goes working); every later turn phase, the pid_bind, and the teardown then pin to t1_start's session via same_session_as, so a co-occurring pre-session can never satisfy them. PER-TURN TOKEN ACCUMULATION (the scenario's `TotalTokens > 0` signal) isn't expressible in the phase DSL today (no metric form in the invariant DSL); aider's `cost_tracking: false` means only sent/received token snapshots are carried, no provider dollar cost — covered out-of-band by parser unit tests (TestParser_MultiTurn_EmitsAssistantMessagePerTurn). HOW runs differ: exact assistant wording, per-turn token counts, and sub-second timing vary (model-output variance). Stable structurally: three ready→working→ready arcs on one session, a single process_exited, and a final ready state. NOTE: this spec is the DOCUMENTED TARGET — validated when aider auth is available in-env (no provider key present at authoring time; headless aider hangs on OpenRouter OAuth).

text: A session appears in ready within 2s of REPL launch (the proc-<PID> pre-session; the lazy transcript has not materialized yet)
First prompt → working (binds the live aider session; the pre-session never enters working)
First turn ends → back to ready (IdleFlush synthesizes turn-end after the prompt goes quiet); state reset between turns, never entered waiting
Second user prompt → working again on the same session
Second turn ends → back to ready
Third user prompt → working again on the same session
Third turn ends in ready; session never enters waiting across the conversation
PID bound to the live aider session
REPL torn down once after the third turn → a single process_exited on the session; state immediately before teardown is ready
